reasoning: The 30-day average of protests over the 360 days preceding March 30, 2025 was 0.083, which equates to exactly 1 protest over the entire year (1 protest / 12 months = 0.083). Therefore, the base rate for observing at least one protest in any given 30-day window is approximately 8.3%. For the first resolution date (April 6), the 30-day window is March 7 to April 5. An extensive search yielded no reports of protests in Kuwait between March 7 and March 30. While a minor ACLED-recorded event could have slipped under the radar, the lack of news reports drops the likelihood below the base rate for the first period. Subsequent periods see a return to the natural base rate, smoothed upwards towards the 0.5 maximum uncertainty line due to the increasing forecast horizon as per instructions. Political tensions remain following the Emir's dissolution of parliament in May 2024, but Kuwait consistently maintains a very low ongoing protest incidence compared to historical spikes in 2011–2012.
